messages.content: Tell me a random fun fact about the Roman Empire

Ancient Romans used **urine** to whiten their teeth.

Because urine contains ammonia (a powerful cleaning agent), they believed it was excellent for lifting stains. It was so in demand for laundry and dental hygiene that vessels were placed on street corners for people to relieve themselves in, and the contents were collected and sold.

In fact, it became such a lucrative business that Emperor Vespasian actually placed a **tax on urine**. When his son complained that taxing pee was disgusting, Vespasian held up a gold coin and famously said, *"Pecunia non olet"* ("Money does not stink").
